answer:
\boxed{B}  

The audit showed that while epidural use increased significantly, it did not reach all women, as only 51% of labors received it by the last trimester. Additionally, the hierarchical clustering analysis revealed that not all women achieved optimal pain relief, with one cluster (226 patients) still reporting moderate pain (VAS 4.1) after epidural. Thus, the standard of effective analgesia was not universally reachable.
